selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential risks and take proactive steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverified content, and fact-check before sharing. 2) Use third-party fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of news articles and other information you come across. 3) Set your social media platforms to warn you about potentially false content, so you can make informed decisions about what you interact with. 4) Use privacy settings to control who can contact you or share content with you. 5) Be mindful of the language and tone used in fake content, as it may be designed to elicit an emotional response. By following these tips, you can minimize the risk of encountering and spreading misinformation on social media."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential risks and take proactive steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or unverified content, and fact-check before sharing. 2) Use third-party fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of news articles and other information you come across. 3) Set your social media platforms to warn you about potentially false content, so you can make informed decisions about what you interact with. 4) Use privacy settings to control who can contact you or share content with you. 5) Be mindful of the language and tone used in fake content, as it may be designed to elicit an emotional response. By following these tips, you can minimize the risk of encountering and spreading misinformation on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential risks and take proactive steps to protect yourself. These include being cautious of sensational or unverified content, using third-party fact-checking tools, setting privacy settings, and being mindful of the language and tone used in fake content.
